test: CT thorax and abdomen with IV contrast

diagnosis.primary: Non-small cell lung carcinoma (adenocarcinoma) of the left upper lobe

procedures.procedure: Bronchoscopy with EBUS-TBNA and transbronchial biopsy

findings.lungs: Postoperative scarring changes in the left upper lobe after partial resection; unchanged paraseptal bulla in the right lower lobe with wall thickening; no new intrapulmonary focal lesions.
findings.lymph_nodes: No significantly enlarged mediastinal, hilar, or axillary lymph nodes; reactive inguinal lymph nodes up to 0.8 cm.
findings.cardiovascular: Mild coronary sclerosis; no pericardial effusion; normal-sized heart and mediastinum.
findings.spine_bone: Osteochondrosis at C6/7; dorsal spondylodesis with internal fixator from L3 to S1 with intact hardware; PEEK cage at L3/4 and titanium cages at L4/5 and L5/S1.
findings.biliary_system: No dilation of intra- or extrahepatic bile ducts; gallbladder well filled without stones.
findings.pancreas: Normal size and structure; no dilation of the Wirsung duct.
findings.kidneys: Orthotopic, normal size, symmetric contrast uptake; no urinary tract obstruction.
findings.gastrointestinal: Unremarkable stomach and small bowel; stool-filled colon.
findings.pelvis: Well-filled bladder; normal rectum, uterus, and adnexa.

assessment: Postoperative changes after partial resection of the left upper lobe with no evidence of metastases.
notes: Comprehensive CT evaluation of thorax and abdomen demonstrating stable postoperative lung findings without evidence of recurrence or metastasis, with additional findings of hepatomegaly and prior spinal fixation.
